Petition For Removal Of Dillon Wong As Co-Trustee, Account, & Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-25-2015 Continued To Jun-29-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Malachowski for (Petioner) Anderson Wong.

Mr. Malachowski reported that discovery, production of documents, special interrogatories and a formal accounting are still due, that there was a Motion to Compel on calendar and the deposition of the property manager was taken but nothing was resolved. Mr. Malachowski stated that he sent a letter to meet and confer to no avail, will be filing a Motion to Compel on the remaining matters and that both parties have agreed to use Bay Properties to manage the house. The Petition for Removal of Dillon Wong as Co-Trustee, etc., was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on June 29, 2015 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court). The signed order was handed to Mr. Malachowski in court and will be mailed to Mr. Spalding.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petitioner Anderson Wongs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Discovery Responses, Accounting, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Continued To Oct-08-14 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Dillon Wong As Co-Trustee, Account, & Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Continued To Nov-12-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Malachowski for (petitioner) Anderson Wong. Charlie Yu for (respondent) Dillon Wong.

Mr. Malachowski stated that he never received an accounting or answers to the special interrogatories regarding property expenses and that he was told at mediation that the had no documents/records. Mr. Malachowski also stated that they have agreed to depose the property manager (Tim Franklin), that they have agreed to hire Bay Properties to manage the property (single family unit) and that he needs to get keys from Mr. Yu. Mr. Yu explained that all documents were with the property manager, that they requested them and received nothing. In addition Mr. Malachowski stated that there is no dispute regarding the securities that they have been reinvested and should be distributed. Mr. Yu stated that there is an informal accounting, that Anderson has amounts from Tim as to what was spent on the property but has no hard documents. The petition for removal of co-trustee, etc. was continued to November 12, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Removal Of Dillon Wong As Co-Trustee, Account, & Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-28-2014 Continued To Aug-06-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Malachowski for Anderson Wong. Charlie Yu for Dillon Wong (Respondent).

Mr. Malachowski stated that there is rental property worth about 1.1 million, that there are $880,000.00 in stocks and securities, and personal property in the amount of $3,000.00. Mr. Malachowski also stated that the Trustees can't sell the property, that they are allowed to take rents, that they are to maintain the property and hold it in Trust for the next generation, that the Trust says that Dillon and Anderson can act together, and that there have been no tax filings in quite a while (he thinks since 1995). Mr. Malachowski stated that he would like an accounting back to 2005. An informal accounting is to be produced by June 27, 2014. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than August 1, 2014. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by August 1, 2014. The Petition for Removal of Co-Trustees, etc. was continued to and the Parties are to return on August 6, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
